message.content: {"items":[{"title":"British Household Panel Survey, Waves 1-8, 1991-1998","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.4399","justification":"BHPS covers the UK in the 1990s with rich panel data on family life and marriage, social stratification, economic conditions, political attitudes, and social behaviour, matching multiple requested themes."},{"title":"International Social Survey Programme: Religion II - ISSP 1998","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.4395","justification":"This ISSP 1998 module focuses on religion and values and includes UK respondents, directly addressing religion and values as well as social attitudes in the late 1990s."},{"title":"International Social Survey Programme: Role of Government III - ISSP 1996","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.4393","justification":"The 1996 ISSP Role of Government module includes the UK and examines political behaviour and attitudes, views on government, and political systems, fitting the government and political themes for the mid‑1990s."},{"title":"International Social Survey Programme: Family and Changing Gender Roles II - ISSP 1994","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.4391","justification":"This 1994 ISSP module includes the UK and focuses on family life and marriage, gender roles, and related social attitudes, matching the family and social behaviour themes."},{"title":"International Social Survey Programme: Social Inequality II - ISSP 1992","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.4389","justification":"The 1992 ISSP Social Inequality module includes the UK and is directly about social stratification and groupings, including attitudes toward inequality, class, and economic distribution in the early 1990s."},{"title":"International Social Survey Programme: Environment I - ISSP 1993","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.4390","justification":"This 1993 ISSP module covers environmental attitudes and related social and political behaviour with UK participation, contributing to social behaviour and political attitudes in the 1990s context."},{"title":"International Social Survey Programme: National Identity I - ISSP 1995","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.4392","justification":"The 1995 ISSP National Identity module includes UK data on political and social attitudes, national belonging, and views of government and society, aligning with political behaviour and social values themes."},{"title":"International Social Survey Programme: Religion I - ISSP 1991","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.4388","justification":"This 1991 ISSP Religion module includes the UK and focuses on religion and values, plus related social attitudes, fitting the religion and values theme in the early 1990s."},{"title":"International Social Survey Programme: Work Orientations II - ISSP 1997","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.4394","justification":"The 1997 Work Orientations module includes UK respondents and examines work values, economic attitudes, and aspects of social stratification and economic development in the late 1990s."},{"title":"International Social Survey Programme: Social Networks II - ISSP 1998","url_or_doi":"https://dbk.gesis.org/dbksearch/sdesc2.asp?no=3430&db=e&doi=10.4232/1.3430","justification":"This 1998 ISSP module, with UK participation, investigates social networks, social behaviour, and support structures, relating to social behaviour and family‑like support systems in the 1990s UK."}]}
